login.title: Welcome back
login.subtitle: Sign in to your account to continue
login.brandSubtitle: Reliable delivery, automatic retries, and complete visibility for every webhook.

register.title: Create your account
register.subtitle: Get started with your webhook infrastructure
register.passwordHint: Must include uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character (@$!%*?&-_#)
register.terms: By creating an account, you agree to our Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.
register.success: Account created! Please verify your email.
register.brandSubtitle: Create your account and send your first webhook event in under 5 minutes.
register.benefit1: Free to start — no credit card required
register.benefit3: Full API access from day one
register.verificationSent: We've sent a verification link to {{email}}. Click it to activate your account.
register.resendHint: Didn't receive it? Check your spam folder or click resend above.

forgotPassword.title: Forgot your password?
forgotPassword.subtitle: Enter your email address and we'll send you a link to reset your password.
forgotPassword.sentMessage: If an account exists for {{email}}, we've sent a password reset link. The link expires in 1 hour.
forgotPassword.tooMany: Too many requests. Please try again later.
forgotPassword.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

resetPassword.title: Set new password
resetPassword.subtitle: Enter your new password below. It must be at least 8 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, a number, and a special character.
resetPassword.successMessage: Your password has been successfully reset. You can now sign in with your new password.
resetPassword.invalidLinkMessage: This password reset link is invalid or missing the required token.
resetPassword.tooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
resetPassword.failed: Failed to reset password. The link may have expired.

verification.banner: Please verify your email {{email}} to unlock all features.

createDialog.description: Add a new webhook endpoint to receive events
createDialog.urlHint: The URL where webhook events will be sent
createDialog.rateLimitHint: Maximum requests per second (leave empty for no limit)

deleteDialog.title: Delete endpoint?
deleteDialog.description: This action cannot be undone. This endpoint will stop receiving webhooks immediately.

toggleDialog.enableDesc: This endpoint will start receiving webhooks again.
toggleDialog.disableDesc: This endpoint will stop receiving webhooks until re-enabled.

rotateDialog.title: Rotate webhook secret?
rotateDialog.description: A new secret will be generated. You'll need to update your webhook signature verification with the new secret. The old secret will stop working immediately.

secretDialog.title: New Webhook Secret
secretDialog.description: Save this secret securely. It won't be shown again.
secretDialog.hint: Use this secret to verify webhook signatures in your application.

testDialog.title: Endpoint Test Result
testDialog.description: Test webhook was sent to the endpoint

deleteDialog.title: Delete Subscription?
deleteDialog.description: This subscription will be permanently deleted. Events of this type will no longer be delivered to the endpoint.

keyDialog.title: API Key Created Successfully

keyDialog.hint: Use this key in the X-API-Key header when sending events

purgeDialog.description: This will permanently delete all {{count}} items from the Dead Letter Queue. This action cannot be undone.

hero.subtitle: Configure endpoints once. We handle delivery, retries, signatures, and monitoring. You focus on building product.

features.title: Everything you need for
features.subtitle: Enterprise-grade reliability with developer-friendly simplicity.
features.retriesDesc: Exponential backoff with configurable retry policies. Never miss a delivery.
features.visibilityDesc: See every attempt, HTTP status, error message, and latency metric in real time.
features.hmacDesc: Every payload signed with HMAC-SHA256. Verify authenticity on your end.
features.replayDesc: Replay any failed delivery from the dashboard. No code changes needed.
features.analyticsDesc: Time-series charts, success rates, latency percentiles, endpoint health scores.
features.mtlsDesc: Configure mutual TLS for endpoints that require client certificate authentication.

architecture.title: Battle-tested
architecture.subtitle: Every event is persisted atomically, published reliably, and delivered with full observability

observability.title: Full visibility into
observability.subtitle: See every attempt with full HTTP context — status codes, error messages, latency, and timestamps.
observability.timelineDesc: View all retry attempts with full HTTP context
observability.errorDetailsDesc: See exact error messages and response bodies
observability.perfMetricsDesc: Track latency percentiles and identify slow endpoints

devExperience.title: Developer-friendly API
devExperience.subtitle: Clean, predictable API. Send events in one request. We handle delivery, retries, and monitoring.
devExperience.accepted: Event accepted · ID: evt_abc123 · 3 deliveries queued

howItWorks.title: How it
howItWorks.subtitle: From first API call to full observability in under 5 minutes
howItWorks.step1Desc: One API call. That's it. We persist atomically and queue for delivery.
howItWorks.step2Desc: HMAC-signed payloads, exponential backoff, automatic retries across multiple intervals.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Full observability dashboard. See every attempt, replay failures, track success rates.

cta.title: Start delivering webhooks reliably
cta.subtitle: Sign up, configure endpoints, and send your first event in under 5 minutes.

quickstartPage.title: From zero to first webhook
quickstartPage.subtitle: Complete walkthrough: create a project, install an SDK, configure endpoints, send events, verify signatures, and monitor deliveries.

step1.title: Create a project
step1.desc: A project organizes your webhooks, endpoints, API keys, and events. Sign up and create your first project from the dashboard, or use the API:

step2.title: Install an SDK
step2.desc: Official SDKs for Node.js, Python, and PHP. Or use the REST API directly with cURL or any HTTP client.

step3.title: Create a webhook endpoint
step3.desc: Register the URL where you want to receive webhooks. Each endpoint gets a unique signing secret for HMAC verification.
step3.saveSecretDesc: The secret is shown only once. Store it securely — you'll need it to verify webhook signatures in Step 6.

step4.title: Subscribe to event types
step4.desc: Link your endpoint to specific event types. Only matching events will be delivered — no noise, no filtering on your side.
step4.dotNotation: You define the event types. Use dot notation for namespacing (e.g. resource.action).

step5.title: Send your first event
step5.desc: Events are sent using your API key (not JWT). The platform routes them to all subscribed endpoints automatically.

step6.title: Verify webhook signatures
step6.desc: Every webhook includes an X-Signature header. Always verify it to ensure the request came from Hookflow, not a third party.

step7.title: Monitor deliveries & replay failures
step7.desc: Every delivery is tracked with full attempt history. If something fails, replay it with one click — no code changes required.
step7.fullVisibilityDesc: See HTTP status, response body, headers, latency, and error messages for every attempt.
step7.oneClickReplayDesc: Replay any failed delivery from the dashboard. Same payload, same endpoint — no code changes.
step7.autoRetriesDesc: Failed deliveries retry automatically with exponential backoff: 30s → 2m → 15m → 1h → 6h. Up to 5 attempts.

sdkSection.title: Official SDKs
sdkSection.subtitle: First-class SDKs with type safety, automatic retries, and signature verification built-in.

finalCta.title: You're all set 🚀
finalCta.subtitle: Your webhook infrastructure is ready. Explore the dashboard, check delivery logs, and invite your team.

overview.title: API Documentation
overview.subtitle: Complete reference for integrating webhook delivery into your application.
overview.whatIsDesc1: Webhook Platform is a reliable webhook delivery service that handles event routing, retries, and monitoring for your application.
overview.whatIsDesc2: Send events via our API, and we'll deliver them to your configured endpoints with automatic retries, signatures, and full visibility.

concepts.eventDesc: A data payload you send to our API that needs to be delivered to webhook endpoints.
concepts.endpointDesc: A URL where webhooks are delivered. Each endpoint has a unique secret for signature verification.
concepts.subscriptionDesc: Links an endpoint to specific event types, controlling which events get delivered where.
concepts.deliveryDesc: A single webhook delivery attempt to an endpoint, with full attempt history and status.
concepts.attemptDesc: Each retry of a delivery, including HTTP status, error message, and latency.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.subtitle: Two authentication methods: JWT for dashboard access and API keys for event ingestion.
auth.jwtDesc: Use JWT tokens to access dashboard endpoints like projects, endpoints, and deliveries.
auth.usingJwtDesc: Include the access token in the Authorization header for all authenticated requests:
auth.apiKeyDesc: Use API keys to send events to the platform. Each project has its own API keys.
auth.securityNoteDesc: Never expose API keys in client-side code or public repositories. Store them securely in environment variables.

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.subtitle: Complete workflow from registration to sending your first webhook.
gettingStarted.step1Desc: Create an account and get your JWT token.
gettingStarted.step2Desc: Projects organize your webhooks, endpoints, and events.
gettingStarted.step3Desc: Generate an API key to send events.
gettingStarted.step4Desc: Add a URL where you want to receive webhooks.
gettingStarted.step5Desc: Subscribe the endpoint to specific event types.
gettingStarted.step6Desc: Send your first event using the API key.

eventsApi.title: Events API
eventsApi.subtitle: Send events that will be delivered to subscribed webhook endpoints.
eventsApi.sendEventDesc: Send an event to be delivered to all subscribed endpoints. Events are processed asynchronously and queued for delivery.
eventsApi.idempotencyDesc: Use the Idempotency-Key header to safely retry requests. If the same key is sent within 24 hours, the original response will be returned without creating duplicate events.

endpointsApi.title: Endpoints API
endpointsApi.subtitle: Manage webhook endpoints where events are delivered.
endpointsApi.createDesc: Add a new webhook endpoint to receive events.
endpointsApi.listDesc: Get all endpoints for a project.
endpointsApi.deleteDesc: Deletes an endpoint and all its subscriptions.
endpointsApi.rotateSecretDesc: Generate a new webhook secret for an endpoint.
endpointsApi.testDesc: Sends a test webhook to verify endpoint connectivity.

subscriptionsApi.title: Subscriptions API
subscriptionsApi.subtitle: Route specific event types to endpoints.
subscriptionsApi.createDesc: Subscribe an endpoint to event types.
subscriptionsApi.listDesc: Get all subscriptions for a project.

deliveriesApi.title: Deliveries API
deliveriesApi.subtitle: Monitor and manage webhook deliveries.
deliveriesApi.listDesc: Get all deliveries for a project with filters.
deliveriesApi.getAttemptsDesc: View all retry attempts for a delivery.
deliveriesApi.bulkReplayDesc: Re-send multiple failed deliveries at once. Filter by status, endpoint, or provide specific delivery IDs.

security.title: Webhook Security
security.subtitle: Verify webhook authenticity using HMAC signatures.
security.sigVerificationDesc: Every webhook includes an X-Signature header with an HMAC-SHA256 signature.
security.endpointVerificationDesc: When you register an endpoint, we send a verification challenge to confirm you own the URL.
security.challengeRequestDesc: We POST a JSON payload with type webhook.verification:
security.expectedResponseDesc: Return the challenge value in your response:

errors.title: Errors & Rate Limits
errors.subtitle: HTTP status codes, error responses, and rate limiting.
errors.rateLimitingDesc: The Events API is rate limited to protect the platform. Rate limit information is included in response headers.
errors.rateLimitExceededDesc: When rate limited, wait until X-RateLimit-Reset timestamp before retrying.
errors.validationErrorsDesc: When request validation fails, you'll receive detailed field-level errors.

sdks.title: SDKs
sdks.subtitle: Official client libraries for Node.js, Python, and PHP — with full TypeScript / type-hint support.
